name: Route of the 3Cs: Miravet Castle, Benifallet Caves, and the Wine Cathedral
name_original: La Cubeta de Mora – vuelta por el castillo de Miravet y la Catedral del Vi
description: This moderate 27.3-mile mountain bike route explores La Cubeta de Mora, featuring Miravet Castle and the Cathedral of Wine.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Móra D'Ebre offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Ebro River and the foothills of the Sierra de Cavalls. The region features varied terrain, from riverbanks to hilly sections, providing a mix of challenges for mountain bikers. This area is characterized by its natural features and an extensive network of trails. The geography includes significant elevation changes and technical features, making it suitable for different biking styles. Best mountain bike trails in Móra D'Ebre The most popular mountain bike trail is Route of the 3Cs: Miravet Castle, Benifallet Caves, and the Wine Cathedral, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete, featuring historical landmarks like Miravet Castle. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Móra d'Ebre Bridge – Castle of Móra d’Ebre loop from Móra d'Ebre, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.8 km) path that includes views of the Ebro River and the castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Miravet Ferry Crossing – Miravet — Village and Riverside loop from Móra d'Ebre, a 22.3 miles (35.9 km) trail leading through riverside areas and the picturesque village of Miravet, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Móra D'Ebre is defined by the Ebro River, the Sierra de Cavalls, and varied hilly landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Móra D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from 39 reviews. More than 583 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Móra D'Ebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Móra D'Ebre?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Móra D'Ebre, offering a diverse range of experiences from riverside paths to challenging climbs in the Sierra de Cavalls. The komoot community has explored more than 580 routes in the area.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Móra D'Ebre?
A: The trails around Móra D'Ebre cater to various skill levels. You'll find 13 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for beginners and experienced riders alike. The terrain includes everything from gentle riverbanks to technical sections with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Móra D'Ebre?
A: The terrain is highly varied, shaped by the Ebro River and the foothills of the Sierra de Cavalls. You can expect a mix of riverbank paths, hilly sections with challenging climbs and descents, and technical features. Some trails, like those near Bikepark Fraus, are known for clayey sections, zig-zag berms, and wooden and stone drops.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, Móra D'Ebre offers several easy mountain bike trails suitable for families. These often follow the Ebro River or explore less challenging terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5318440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Móra d'Ebre loop from Móra d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.8 km route that provides a pleasant ride with historical views.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Many trails offer views of significant landmarks and natural features. You can ride past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5134983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Móra d’Ebre&lt;/a&gt;, explore the picturesque village of Miravet and its imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or even experience the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5129643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Ferry Crossing&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4456948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Blai&lt;/a&gt; is another notable point of interest along some routes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Móra D'Ebre?
A: The trails in Móra D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of challenging and scenic routes, and the historical points of interest along the way. The variety of terrain, from river views to mountain climbs, is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Móra D'Ebre are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Móra d'Ebre Bridge – Castle of Móra d’Ebre loop from Móra d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Ferry Crossing – Miravet — Village and Riverside loop from Móra d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Móra D'Ebre is suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round activity, including options for winter fat biking.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife or enjoy nature reserves while mountain biking?
A: While directly on the mountain bike trails, you'll experience the natural beauty of the Ebro River and surrounding hills. For dedicated wildlife viewing, the nearby Sebes Nature Reserve (north of Flix) is excellent for birdwatching, and the broader Ebro Delta Natural Park offers rich flora and fauna, including flamingos, though these are typically accessed separately from the main MTB trails.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the mountain bike trails in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Móra D'Ebre is accessible by public transport, primarily by bus services connecting it to larger towns and cities in Catalonia. While public transport can get you to the town, reaching specific trailheads might require further planning or a short ride from the town center, as many trails start directly from or near Móra D'Ebre itself.

Q: Where can I find parking near the mountain bike trailheads in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Parking is generally available within Móra D'Ebre town, particularly near the Ebro River or the castle area, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Specific trailheads outside the town might have limited parking, so it's advisable to check local maps or komoot route details for designated parking areas.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer significant elevation changes for a challenge?
A: Yes, the region's mountainous terrain, particularly in the foothills of the Sierra de Cavalls and around peaks like La Picosa, provides trails with significant elevation gains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38245808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jeroni Hermitage – Castle of Móra d’Ebre loop from Móra d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; feature over 700 meters of ascent, offering a good challenge for experienced riders.
tips: Old military building of Islamic origin located within the municipality of Móra d'Ebre, in Ribera d'Ebre. The castle enclosure, which is 54 meters high, was walled to defend against possible attacks.

The "Catedral del vi" or cathedral of wine is a modernist building built by a disciple of Gaudi, Cèsar Martinell, at the beginning of the 20th century. This is a cooperative that continues to produce wine, olive oil and other regional products. The visit is definitely worth it!

Miravet Castle is located in a strategic point dominating the course of the Ebro river and the adjacent territory. An imposing fortress with spectacular views. The castle stands on a hill and is protected by a wall up to 25 m high, which has witnessed some of the most significant moments in this country.
https://patrimoni.gencat.cat/es/monumentos/monumentos/castillo-de-miravet
The pier located on the Ebro River, a few meters from Miravet, offers incredible views of the town and the castle that rises on top of the hill. A beautiful and typical postcard of Miravet.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated riding time for this route is approximately 3.5 hours. However, plan for a longer overall duration to account for stops at points of interest like the Miravet Castle and the Cathedral of Wine of Pinell de Brai, as well as enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. The terrain in the La Cubeta de Mora basin features a mix of limestone elevations, suggesting rocky or gravelly paths in higher sections, and flatter, possibly alluvial or riverside dirt tracks along the Ebro River. You can expect some climbs and diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is designed as a circular tour, allowing you to start and finish at the same point after exploring the area around Miravet Castle and the Cathedral of Wine.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several significant landmarks, including the Castle of Móra d’Ebre, the impressive Miravet Castle perched above the Ebro River, and the unique Cathedral of Wine of Pinell de Brai. There's also a fantastic View of Miravet and its castle from the Ebro River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn are generally the best times for mountain biking. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is often at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler and potentially wetter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in this region, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or in protected natural zones. Be sure to carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits are required to ride this mountain bike route itself. However, if you plan to enter any of the castles or the Cathedral of Wine, there may be separate entrance fees for those attractions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, you can typically find parking in the towns of Móra d’Ebre or Miravet, which are key points along the route. Look for public parking areas within these towns to start your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll ride parts of El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, E39, E38, and the Camí dels Brois, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery can I expect?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse natural beauty, characterized by the unique geographical features of La Cubeta de Mora. You'll experience picturesque views of the meandering Ebro River, surrounding limestone hills, and potentially agricultural areas and vineyards, especially near the 'Cathedral of Wine'.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The towns of Móra d’Ebre and Miravet, which are part of or close to the route, offer options for cafes, restaurants, and other amenities where you can refuel and refresh during your ride.

View of Miravet and its castle from the Ebro River
